Tokyo ! It's a good bet that the Japanese yen will continue falling in the next five months so you might as well go on holiday in Japan over the easter holiday. I enjoy visiting the annual spring carnival where I ride the thrill rides amd eat sushi, soaking up the nice christmassy atmosphere. The sun has been dazzlingly bright so you may just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onsen, soaking up the sun. In fact, I've just got out of an onsen and am drying myself on a towel and getting dressed. I remember two decades ago, there was a protest where the protestors clashed with the police in Osaka. I saw it on NHK then. I thought the police weren't tough enough. All the riot police were holding a shield in one hand and a truncheon in the other hand. In this way, you can't really fight the protestors and arrest them since you have no free hands to grab ahold of them. That's why it took four policemen to catch hold of one demonstrator. From a fighting perspective, some of the riot police should be holding just a shield, some just a nightstick, the others both. And all of them should be trained as to how they fight when they're holding just a shield, holding just a baton and holding both. It was absolute chaos. I didn't think Osaka had ever been so disastrous before. The police who I wholeheartedly supported only used tear smoke in an attempt to drive the demonstrators away which was clearly not enough. The police shouldn't have been afraid of being criticized for using excessive force because this was utterly not the case. Firing rubber bullets and bean bags at the protestors was necessary lawful and appropriate. Those who sabotaged the nearby buildings amd the railings should've been sent to jail. The train network was partially paralyzed for many weeks. The world had brought the city to the edge of a catastrophe. The United States had already imposed trade sanctions on Japan at that time. The then United States president said he would take punitive measures against Japan if the police there used excessive force. Japan was afraid that America would toughen punitive import duties still further on alcohol, agricultural products, food and clothes and worse still, they would convince other nations to impose international economic sanctions against the country. Of course, the downside was the costs of the products which they had imposed punitive import tariffs on would rise still higher. The economic sanctions against Japan wouldnt be lifted until the then US president's term of office expired a year later. The next day everybody was warned of the danger of violent clashes around the place only to discover that it was a false alarm. There were half as many people on the street. However, as long as you kept up with the news amd stayed away from places where there were protests, Osaka was safe. In the same evening, a bus crash killed twenty three passengers. Detectives assigned the blame for the accident to a hailstorm and heavy snowfalls - and then a brutal murder took place in the capital. Detectives assigned jealousy amd hatred as the motives for the horrendous crime.

replies.msg: 下年先問

There's a pageant of which the final round will take place next month in Tokyo. I would've thought that Aiko is highly likely to enter the final. My cousin said "I'll bet Fumi will enter the final." My friend Alex has received a commission to compose a waltz for thr pageant as one of the contestants will be showcasing her waltz dance which we all can't wait to see. Let me offer him my congratulations for having accepted a commission for a waltz from the pageant since it's a rare opportunity. I too offer heartfelt congratulations to my friend Kawai in having progressed through the preliminary round. She's a contestant in the beauty contest and I won't be surprised if she progresses through the semifinal.
